Now, May 6, 1965, something amazing happened. For decades and decades prior to that, human beings believed that it was physically impossible to break the four-minute mile. But on that day, May 6, 1965, Roger Bannister did it. Now, what's interesting is, again, decades, centuries prior to that, nobody believed it was possible. But as soon as Roger Bannister did it, people thought it was possible. In fact, just 46 days later, John Landy, because he saw what Roger Bannister did, he thought, if he can do it, I can do it. And just 46 days, he did it. And he broke the four-minute mile as well. And since then, dozens and dozens of people have done it. But it wasn't until one person did it that everyone else believed it was possible. August 17, 2004 was the day the four-minute mile was broken for me. At the time, I was a young kid. I just learned about this whole internet thing. I was geeking out. I was making about $1,000 a month. I thought I was the smartest guy in the world. And on August 17, I was 24 years old at the time, this man, John Reese, he set out to do something that nobody thought was possible. He said, I'm going to make a million dollars in a day. And he went out there and he put together the internet campaign, put in a really cool funnel together. He launched it. And in just 18 hours, they passed the million-dollar mark. Now me, as a 24-year-old kid, is sitting there looking at this with my goals and aspirations were to make $1,000 a month online. And I was like, John Reese, if this guy who's just like me, just like any of us, can make a million dollars in a day, I wonder if I can make a million dollars in a year. First time I'd ever crossed my mind. I was like, I don't even know if that's possible. But if John can do a million dollars in a day, I think I could do a million dollars in a calendar year. That was my goal. And so I started on January 1, 2005. I said, OK, this is the year. I'm going to make it in a calendar year. And I launched all these different things. And in 12 months, I missed the goal. I didn't hit a million dollars in sales. I got close, but I didn't hit it. It was 2005. 2006, I tried again. January 1st, I was going to do a million dollars in the next 12 months. I launched a bunch of stuff again. And I didn't make it again. And then the next year, it was January 2007, I launched it. And by October 16th, I crossed the million dollar mark in a calendar year. Thank you. Because John did it, I knew that I could do it. Now, since then, I've gone out and had multiple million dollars years. I had a million dollar months and even a million dollar days. But it wasn't until I knew that John could do it that I could do it. Now, for my community, in fact, how many funnel hackers do we have out here in the audience right now? Yeah, my people. I love it. So for my audience, I was like, when I believed, when I saw John Reiss doing, I had belief that I could do it. It changed everything for me. So those of you who don't know me, my name is Russell Brunson. I am a serial entrepreneur with an obsession of helping entrepreneurs around the world. Nothing gets me more excited than that. So this is something that is kind of on the new side, but man, it's starting to get more and more mainstream. The more that I talk about this, okay. My entire goal is to get everybody to understand that sales funnels are the key to growing all of our companies. Okay. So what is a sales funnel? For those who don't know, let me start actually with what you probably do know. Most of us know what a traditional website is, right? Okay. I always call this the anti-sales department. We send people these traditional websites and what it's like, it's kind of like having a salesperson there and when somebody comes, they hand them a brochure and then they step back and they hope and they pray that the sale gets made. Now how many of you guys know if you had a salesperson who was handing out brochures and then hiding, you would fire them instantly? Yeah. Yeah. For some reason we're okay with our websites doing nothing, right? Okay. For all of us who are entrepreneurs, who are spending our own money out of our own pockets to grow our companies, we have to be smarter. We have to build something where we put a dollar in advertising in and instantly get two dollars back out. Okay. And that is what a sales funnel is. A sales funnel is kind of like taking your very best salesperson that you have, cloning that person and making it so that every single person that comes to your website, they grab them, they introduce themselves, they find out what they're interested in taking that to buy the actual product and service that they need to help grow and have more success. Okay. That is what a sales funnel is. Much better way to do it. Okay. Now after 10 years of doing this business, you guys, I've tried a whole bunch of crazy stuff. I'm going to break it all down. Very simple, very easy. And so you guys, because you're here today and you woke up this morning, you get a cheat and get it the easy way. How many of you guys want it the easy way? Yeah. All right. So let me show you the hard way because funnels used to be hard. So nobody ever did them for the first decade of my game because I was in this game because they were hard to do. Okay. This is a sales funnel. I'm going to show you guys behind the scenes of this one. This is one of my supplement companies that we sold last year. Okay. Now to build this funnel, it doesn't look that complicated. I took pictures and put them on my walks. I wanted to see what it all looked like. It's only what one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, eight pages. Yeah. That's probably pretty easy. Probably takes an hour or two, right? No, this is what we had to go through to create this. So step number one to create a funnel is we need to design the funnel, like the strategy, what's it going to be like? What's the upsells and the downsells and the price points to figure that out? Right. Number two, I had to write the copy. The copy is the sales letter. Now our supplement was helping people diabetic neuropathy. I didn't know what that meant. In fact, I made a rule inside of my company. I didn't want anyone to explain to me what diabetic neuropathy was. So we made at least a million dollars in the funnel. So I had no idea what we were selling other than when we knew it worked. And so I hired a copywriter to write the sales letter. It cost $20,000 for the sales letter. Then we had our designer to design the sales letter and a website designer to put online, a programmer to hook the shopping carts and the auto responders to get it all working, analytics person. My upfront cost to build this one funnel alone was $29,000. Okay. Now that's not too bad. The worst thing though is that I had already ordered a supplement. They were sitting in a warehouse gathering dust for eight weeks. That's all I took is to build it two months for me to build this funnel. The way chance to find launch it and it was, it was awesome. But that's why most people have struggled in this business for so long. So with the note sheet, I'm going to dive right in here to secret number one. This week, take notes as I'm going through here. Okay. So I'm going to cover three secrets of funnel hacking. Secret number one. If you guys understand this, this will give you the ability to get unlimited leads for free for the rest of your life. I'm going to show you guys that whoever can spend the most money to acquire customer wins, this is the foundation of funnels. And when you understand this, everything else becomes really, really easy. Whoever can spend the most money to acquire customer wins. Number two, I want to show you guys how to get the best salesperson on planet earth to close sales for your product 24 hours a day, seven days a week. And the best thing about the salesperson is they never complain. They never ask for raise. They never take a break. Now I am a salesperson, just like most of you in this room. And what I've noticed about most of us salespeople, we're kind of high maintenance. We always want to get paid more. We want better leads. We want more stuff. Right. Okay. The salesperson about to introduce you guys to doesn't do any of that stuff and it works 24 hours a day, seven days a week. And the last thing I'm going to show you is how to get all of the customers who are currently going to your competitors start coming to you instead. And it's going to be really fun. So let's dive right in. So secret number one, whoever can spend the most money to acquire a customer wins. Now this is the key to understand you guys. I learned from my first mentor is Dan Kennedy. He said that business beats this competition by making the exact same prospect worth more to his business than to that of his competitors. Whoever can spend the most money to acquire customer wins. I'm going to jump into this to understand. So I launched my company, ClickFunnels, about three years ago. When we did it, it was me and a couple of dudes in an office that we were a little tiny office and all we had was a vision of how to change the world. That was it. Okay. We had no money. We had no venture back cap. We had nothing at that. We just had a dream and a vision. Okay. Now as we were developing this company, this is us literally white boarding ClickFunnels on the wall and these guys coding it while I was just getting excited because I don't know how to code. I'm just excited all the time. And they were working on it. At the time, we're like, well, who's our competitors? Who are we competing against? And I found these are our two competitors. The first competitor got $38 million in funding and the second one got $127 million in funding. And I'm like, all right, well, that's what I'm going up against. I literally have a visa with a thousand dollar limit on it and a dream and a vision. How am I going to win? Okay. All these guys can completely outspend me. Yet we were able to take ClickFunnels from zero to over $100 million in sales in just three years, demolishing both of those two competitors without taking on a penny of outside capital. Thank you. Thank you. Now, since we started doing this, we have a whole bunch of venture capitalists who keep coming to like Russell, come and give you money. I'm like, no, why would I take your money? They, I don't, I want my customers to give me money. That's the way business should happen. And they keep trying to give us money. And finally one day I was just curious. I'm like, what did they kept talking about valuations? Like, well, I want to see what they think we're actually worth. I had no idea. Like this is a whole world that's beyond me. I just want to sell stuff. And so we went to lunch, one of these VCs, and they start asking all these questions. And he said, okay, how do you like, how much does it cost to acquire customer? That's like, that's question number one, right? If you guys watch Shark Tank, they always ask like, what's the cost to acquire a customer? And I was like, well, when we drive ads, we buy a bunch of ads and we drive them to ClickFunnels.com. It cost us about $120 to get a free trial. And the guy was like, like licking his chops. He's like, okay, so if we gave you $50 million in funding, that equals X amount of customers and do all the math in his head. And I was like, whoa, slow down, slow down. I was like, we actually turned those ads off. He's like, what? Why would you turn those ads off? Like you're acquiring customers, $120 a person. That's awesome. I was like, because I have to pay for those. Like I have to pull my wallet out and pay for everyone. Those customers. Like I, I can't float $120 grant, $120 for every free, every free customer who signs up. And he's like, so what do you do? And I said, well, I didn't want to be broke. So we had to do things different. We had to think smarter about things. So I said, this is how we do it. We run ads and I don't sell ClickFunnels up front. Instead, what I do is I sell, I drive people to a sales funnel. And I've got a whole bunch of sales from about five or six sales funnels we use right now. We drive people through and we sell different things. How many of you guys have got, have purchased one of my books? All right. You guys have been through the funnel then. Okay. On average right now, it costs about $20 in ads to sell one copy of my book. Now you guys know, I give my, give away my book for free plus shipping. But with cool thing is that when someone comes by, as they go through this funnel. So that means there's an upsell, there's a downsell, the audio book, I've sold training program, a bunch of things like that. So right now on average, I spend $20 on ads to sell a book and I make about $40. So what just happens is that I get $20 cash net profit into my pocket and I get a customer and then I tell the customer, hey, there's really cool software called ClickFunnels. You should try it. And they try it for free. And it doesn't not, not only does not cost me $120 a customer, I got paid $20 before I even told that customer about what I'm trying to sell them. He was getting this. So the VC didn't. I explained it once. I spent a second time. He's like, wait, wait, I don't understand. So people pay you to become a lead. And then they read your book and they're completely indoctrinated in your belief system and then they sign up and it doesn't cost you anything. I'm like, yeah, it's really awesome. And he's like three or four times. And finally, I remember the very last time he said, if what you're saying is true, this will change businesses. We know it forever. Said, yeah, that's my mission. That's what I'm trying to tell people. You understand this one funnel was worth more to me than $127 million in funding. We blew past both of these companies in less than three years. That's the power sales funnels. All right. Thank you. So this is the secret, guys. When you have a funnel that's created the right way, you can instantly outspend your competitors and ethically still all potential customers in your market. Even with these, my competitors dumping as much cash as they can, my ads are still burying them. They're everywhere. How many of you guys have seen my ads every single time you turn on anything? Hey, tonight when you go to your hotel room, turn on the TV. We bought all the ads on TV as well. Everyone who's in the room block here, you will see my ads all over TV as well. We can now spend everybody. Okay. That's the key. So the question for all of you guys, lots of different business types, and there's 9,000 people, you all have different businesses. The question I'm going to get from most of you guys is what kind of funnel should I use? It depends on the kind of business you have. So I kind of went through and we segmented before I came here to figure out all different markets you guys are in. We broke down about 10. Okay. The first one are people that either run an agency or they're a freelancer. How many of you guys does that fit who you are? There we go. Number two, people selling information products. Number three, see people selling e-commerce products. Number four, people doing coaching or consulting. Number five, people that are network marketing. Number six, local businesses doing anything in chiropractic dental. You know that a lot of business to business people just looking for leads to generate. We've got a whole bunch of people who are bloggers or affiliate marketers. We've got nonprofits and then we got a bunch of people who are just getting started. Is that kind of stuff? All you guys, does that cover everybody? Hopefully there's no one I missed, right? So I want you to understand that you guys remember the commercial from back in the nineties when the iPhone first came out and they're like, there's an app for that. Oh, you need this? There's an app for that. Okay. Just like there's an app for that. I promise you there's a funnel for that. Whatever business you're in, there's a funnel for that. Okay. So the question then is, how do you know what type of funnel to build? Okay. And we do that. We figure out what that is through a very fun, simple process that we call funnel hacking. That's where you guys all get your new nickname. Okay. So let me explain how funnel hacking works. I learned the first part is I learned from Tony Robbins. Tony Robbins told me, said, if you want to achieve success, you know, all you need to do is find a way to model those who've already succeeded. Right. Now I started thinking back in my career of things I've been good at. And I was a wrestler growing up. How many, how many wrestlers do we have in the room? Yeah. Anyone on a wrestle? I would much rather do that right now up here. If you so. All right, let's go. Okay. So I was a wrestler and when I first got started, I was not only not good, I was bad. My first year I got beat almost every single time. And remember the end of the season, my dad took me to the state tournament and state tournament. One of the guys on my high school team won the state title. It was a three time state champ. My dad videotaped what he did and we watched it. And that whole summer we would watch the video of this guy and my dad would teach me the moves that he was doing. So all summer long I practiced it and practiced and I practiced. I found someone who's good. I modeled them. And in a very short period of time, I went from being a really bad wrestler to being a state champ to going to high school national, taking second place in the country and becoming an All-American, eventually going to college and being one of the top 10 wrestlers in the country. Okay. Not because, thank you. Not because I was an amazing wrestler, but because I found people who are super successful and modeled what worked. When I started business, it was the same thing. I went to college, I went to college to wrestle. People asked my degree and I was like, my degree is in wrestling. I didn't even know the classes were there. I barely graduated. And towards the end of it, I started learning about this internet thing. I didn't take business classes. I didn't take entrepreneur classes. I didn't take any. I just knew that I want to make some money. I jumped online. I started seeing people who were successful. I'm like, man, these people are geniuses. I don't know how to do what they do, but I can see what they're doing. I'm just going to model them. So I started modeling what they were doing. And within a year of graduating from college, I made over a million dollars online. Okay. The same thing is true in sales funnel. You guys don't have to be sales funnel geniuses. You don't have to geek out like I do every single day. All you got to do is look around and find someone in your industry or in a similar industry who's having success and then we model them. Okay. Every time I share this, I get somebody who's like, well, that's cool. Russell, but I got a better way to do it. I got a different thing I can do instead. And I always smile at those people. I say, you can always tell who the pioneers are because they've got arrows in their back and they're lying down face, face down in the dirt. Okay. Um, in this industry, you don't want to be a pioneer. You don't want to go try things. First time I ever got this, I was actually at an event and, uh, there was a guy on stage. His name is Porter Stansberry and Porter worked for a company called the Gora publishing. How many guys know about a Gora? They do over $1.5 billion a year selling financial newsletters, biggest financial newsletter company in the world. Okay. And he says, and Porter's up there on stage. He ran a division called Stansberry research, which was the biggest division at the time and he's going through all his numbers and metrics. He's showing his sales funnels. I'm like a little kid in the back like, I can't believe he's telling me this stuff. I was freaking out, right? Taking notes and everything. He's talking a million miles a minute. And all of a sudden he stopped. He said, you know what's interesting? He said, every single year, there's people who see what we're doing here at a Gora publishing and they want to, they want to compete against us. So he said, the first thing they come out and they try to beat us on pricing. They come in and they sell their product for cheaper. He's like, we know from decades of doing this, that you cannot make those numbers work. The metrics just don't work. He says, so then they figure that out eventually, they try something different. They try three or four things. Eventually they go bankrupt and they lose their business. Okay. The biggest thing that kills entrepreneurs is that not that they don't have vision. They do have vision, but usually they either run out of time or money before they have the ability to execute on a vision. Okay. And Porter said at this time, said, if you want to compete with the Gora publishing, you can't try to compete with us. He said, you got to copy us. And that's an light bulb blown off my head. I thought, man, it Gora's got hundreds of full-time copywriters writing, copy, split testing things. If I want to go and try all those things, I don't have the money. I don't have the budget to do that, but I can go and look at like all of the, the, the pioneers that had all the arrows in the back and move over here. Like, where are they today? Oh, they're right here. Oh, that's what's working today. I should just model that. And that's what we do. Okay. That's the way of success so quickly is by finding people who have already succeeded and we model them. Okay. All right. So step number one, this funnel hacking process is to find somebody to funnel hack. So when we launched your supplement company, it's my first supplement company. And we were selling to people who had diabetic neuropathy and I was doing some research like, well, who else out there is having success? There's got to be somebody. And we found this little website right here. And we're going to and looking at, I was like, this is probably the ugliest website I think I've ever seen in my life. It's all purple and black and weird. But I did some research and some, some Intel and I found out about at the time that they're making about a hundred thousand dollars a month, selling a supplement. I was like, okay, cool. They know what they're doing, but I'm a marketing guy. So I'm going to do it better because I know a better way to do it. And then I was like, wait a minute. Maybe they have some arrows in their back. Like, I don't know what people with diabetic neuropathy like. Maybe they know something I don't know. So instead of trying to be creative and do my own thing, I said, look, I'm just going to model what works. So I went out with my very first version of our sales page. It looked very similar. Like, uh, and, uh, one thing I want to stress is that notice that I said model. We didn't copy. Okay. I modeled the look, the field, the layout, the price points. Like they had a big logo on the left hand side. I had a logo. They had a phone number on the right hand side of the phone. They had sales letter, the picture of the bottle. I modeled the look, the field, the layout. It's close as I could. I didn't copy anything, but I modeled the structure. And we launched this in a very short period of time. This little website started making us about $20,000 a month. Okay. Which is cool. And then I was like, oh, there's got to be something else they're doing that I'm, that I'm missing. Like, what is it? How are they having some of success? And so I did what every good entrepreneur has to become good at doing. Pulled my wallet out of my pocket, pulled my credit card out and I bought their product. Like what I do behind the scenes that I'm missing. No one forked anything for me and also for them. They didn't have much on the back end. And it just kind of ended. I was like, God, there's got to be somebody out here knows what they're doing in this industry. So when outside, I started looking for other companies. And eventually I found a supplement company and I met the owner of, and they were doing about $30 million a month selling their supplement. I'm like, okay, this guy knows what he's doing. Went to the website and looked different. It looked like this. There was this video and add the cart button. I'm like, it's kind of weird. I've never seen something like that. And then I was like, okay, I got to see what else they're doing behind the scenes. So I did whatever I could on for never has to become good at doing. And we're looking at thinking, does this guy think I'm a drug addict? That's 10 bottles of this stuff. Like, I don't know how to do that. And at first I was like, this guy's crazy. Like, I'm a marketing guy. I know better how to, a better way to do this. And then luckily for me, I took my pride hung up on the shelf and I said, maybe they've got some arrows on their back. I want to teach you guys something really important. This people always ask me, well, Russell, what do I say on the video? I know how to sell face to face. Like I'm selling someone on the phone. This is why I give you guys this, um, these popsocus. When you put the popsocus on your phone, you pull it out. Okay. Most of my videos today are all done on my phone. I pop out of poxoc. I can hold like this. Okay. This is how it works. And I turn on the camera. And now I can sell everything by answering those four questions. Number one, who are you? Number two, what do you have for your person who's watching this video? Number three, why do they need it? And number four, how can they get it? Easy script in the world and it's magic. It works so good. So I take my phone out. I click record. Said, Hey, my name is Russell Brunson. I'm here today at the 10 X event. I'm really, really excited. What do I have?
